mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced present value of future corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tight labor market with wage pressure and reduced slack, signaling late-cycle conditions that precede equity market corrections. Lower UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations rise and policy tightens further.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while unemployment remains stable, indicating weakening consumer demand without labor market shock. This demand-side deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.3% AND UNRATE stable within ±0.2pp
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (low RSAFS growth + stable UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 113.0 index level while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 207.0, indicating real supply-side expansion without cost-push pressure. This productivity-driven growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1500 thousand units while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains above 137000 thousand, indicating demand weakness in housing without broad labor deterioration. This sector-specific demand shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as construction and related equity exposure contracts.
regime_hint: housing-led demand slowdown with labor resilience
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows consistently above 137500 thousand persons month-over-month, indicating sustained labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as labor-driven income growth sustains equity valuations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 52% of all periods, or Rank IC between PAYEMS level and forward returns is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 206.5 while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 5.5%, indicating real rates are declining and monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This negative real rate regime predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity real returns improve and inflation hedges strengthen.
regime_hint: negative real rates with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 58% of triggered instances, or the real-rate signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates below 11550 billion chained 2017 dollars (quarter-over-quarter annualized below 2.0%) while retail sales (RSAFS) fall below 370000 million dollars, indicating broad demand contraction. This dual demand weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk rises and earnings forecasts decline.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the dual-demand signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
